text:
Fletcher Tringham (フレッチャー・トリンガム, Furecchā Toringamu), also spelt Fletcher Tringum on his reference sheet for the 2003 anime, and his older brother Russell are the sons of the famed alchemist Nash Tringham. Their characters are adapted by the 2003 anime from the light novel Fullmetal Alchemist: The Land of Sand. They seek to complete their father's research with a substance known as "red water," a toxic liquid with alchemical properties. To help them in this endeavor they impersonate the Elric brothers to gain access to resources and locations reserved for State Alchemists. While Fletcher is reluctant to do this, Russell insists that nobody will mind. They are almost executed towards the end of the anime for doing this, and after being saved, they try to help Ed find out about the homunculi. Although Edward and Russell fight near constantly, Fletcher and Alphonse are able to get along. The two Tringham brothers tend to utilize alchemy involving plants.

Trivia

Fletcher and Russell's seiyuu first voiced their characters in the drama CD for the first light novel released in March 2003 a couple months before the first anime's release.
In one of the omakes Arakawa drew for 2003, Fletcher says he's a dog person to Alphonse's fury.
